sections.heading: S
sections.section_value: The patient reports a pain level of 0 on a scale of 0 to 10, with no medication, and describes a worst pain level of 10. He states he was able to get home without assistance but found it painful. He has been taking ibuprofen and elevating/icing his ankle, with some relief. He reports a pain level of approximately 6 when taking ibuprofen.

sections.heading: O
sections.section_value: The patient's vitals are stable with a slightly elevated heart rate. Examination reveals ecchymosis and swelling over the lateral malleolus, tenderness to palpation, and no joint laxity. Capillary refill is brisk, and dorsalis pedis pulses are strong. X-rays show no fracture or bony abnormalities. Neurovascular exam is intact bilaterally.

sections.heading: A
sections.section_value: The patient's symptoms are consistent with an ankle sprain of the lateral ligament complex, likely due to a recent injury. The examination findings support this diagnosis.

sections.heading: P
sections.section_value: I am prescribing an air cast and crutches to stabilize the ankle and limit weight-bearing. I will prescribe NSAIDs to manage pain and swelling. I have scheduled a follow-up appointment in two weeks to monitor progress and assess continued symptoms. I recommend continued elevation, compression, and ice.
